response.justification: Strong senior individual contributor with broad full-stack experience and some recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure (MCP tooling at re:cinq), but the role demands an executive CTO with 5+ years of senior technical leadership, a track record scaling teams from 10 to 30+, and production multi-agent AI systems in regulated fintech. The resume shows no people-management or org-scaling experience, no demonstrated multi-agent production systems, and only tangential fintech exposure (RIDE Capital for ~15 months). The gap between IC craftsman profile and CTO mandate is too wide to bridge in a single hire.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background across web, backend, embedded, and distributed systems with low-latency experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Current hands-on exposure to agentic AI infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq
Strong software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and a public track record of teaching, workshops, and open-source leadership
concerns: No evidence of executive leadership, org scaling (10→30+), or hiring/managing senior technical leaders — resume reads as senior IC throughout
No demonstrated production multi-agent AI systems; agentic work appears recent and tooling-focused rather than architecting stateful, event-driven agent frameworks at scale
Limited fintech-infrastructure depth (one ~15-month stint at RIDE Capital); no credit-risk, real-time trade decisioning, or multi-jurisdiction compliance experience

key_factors.factor: No production multi-agent AI systems experience
Recent agentic AI / MCP exposure at re:cinq
